channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 7 risk factors of deadly fungus infections identified, new Candida auris research finds
item.description: Researchers are getting a better idea of what leads to clinical cases of Candida auris, a dangerous and drug-resistant fungus that has been rapidly multiplying.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: These states approved permanent daylight saving time: Why their clocks still change in November
item.description: These states have all affirmed that they want to observe daylight saving time year-round, yet their clocks will switch to standard time in November with the rest of us. Here's why.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Carolina's longest-serving death row inmate dies after spending 42 years in prison
item.description: Fred Singleton died of natural causes this week after spending over 40 years on South Carolina's death row for the 1982 rape and murder of Elizabeth Lominick.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans try to weaken 50-year-old law protecting whales, seals and polar bears
item.description: BOOTHBAY HARBOR, Maine (AP) — Republican lawmakers are targeting one of the U.S.'s longest standing pieces of environmental legislation, credited with helping save rare whales from extinction.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: As the National Guard enters Memphis, memories of MLK and 1968 unrest resurface
item.description: As the National Guard enters Memphis, some longtime residents are recalling the thousands sent there in 1968.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Mattise And Adrian Sainz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A sacred lake, a Columbus statue, and an Indigenous people's long struggle for land
item.description: GEDDES, N.Y. (AP) — Before white settlers came to Onondaga Lake, before the city of Syracuse grew along its shore, before the pristine waters became one of the most polluted bodies of water in the United States, it was a sacred place for the Onondaga Nation.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Two powerful quakes strike off southern Philippines, killing at least 7 people
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Two powerful offshore earthquakes struck the same region in the southern Philippines hours apart on Friday with the first 7.4 magnitude temblor killing at least seven people, setting off landslides and prompting evacuations of coastal areas nearby because of a brief tsunam
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyers seek dismissal of federal charges in assassination of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lawyers for Luigi Mangione asked a New York federal judge Saturday to dismiss some criminal charges, including the only count for which he could face the death penalty, from a federal indictment brought against him in the December assassination of UnitedHealthcare’s chief executive.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A look at how Venezuelans in the US are reacting to Maria Corina Machado's Nobel Prize win
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — Venezuelans in “Little Venezuela” — the largest home for the country's natives in the United States — are welcoming the news that opposition leader Maria Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Prize with bittersweetness as deportation threats loom.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Bodycam video: Sisters arrested for attacking gate agent at airport
item.description: Newly-released body camera video shows the moment two sisters were arrested for allegedly assaulting a Frontier Airlines gate agent.
item.dc:creator: Sara Kitchin, NewsNation

item.title: Halloween auction for 'The Conjuring' house canceled
item.description: The Halloween auction for the Rhode Island farmhouse made famous by the blockbuster "The Conjuring" has been canceled.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Guernelli, Fox 5

item.title: Nor'easter without a name to bring ocean flooding and high winds to US East Coast
item.description: A storm without a name was expected to bring flooding to the East Coast from South Carolina to New Jersey and high winds to New York City over the holiday weekend. While the storm affecting the eastern U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: National Guard stands down in Illinois while troops patrol in Memphis with local police
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — National Guard troops patrolled in Tennessee's second-largest city for the first time Friday while soldiers in Illinois were engaged only in planning and training after a judge handed a loss to the Trump administration and blocked their deployment to the Chicago area.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WGN employee detained amid ICE activity in Chicago, later released
item.description: An employee of WGN-TV was detained by two Border Patrol officers and later released after federal agents swarmed a busy intersection in Chicago Friday Morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, KRON4

item.title: Trump threatens tech export limits, new 100% tariff on Chinese imports starting Nov. 1 or sooner
item.description: Trump said that “starting November 1st, 2025 (or sooner, depending on any further actions or changes taken by China), the United States of America will impose a Tariff of 100% on China, over and above any Tariff that they are currently paying.
item.dc:creator: KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces 100 percent tariff on China in response to rare earth controls
item.description: President Trump announced Friday he will raise tariffs on China in response to a move from Beijing to tighten its control over certain critical minerals and rare earth elements. Trump announced on Truth Social that he would impose a tariff of 100 percent on Chinese goods beginning Nov. 1 or sooner.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: WGN employee detained amid ICE activity on Chicago's North Side, later released
item.description: WGN-TV is still in the process of searching for and obtaining video showing the moment leading up to the employee's detainment.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, WGN 9

item.title: Cash App giving $100 to users through October. How to enter
item.description: CashApp is giving away $100 to thousands of users every Friday through Oct. 24, as part of its Cash App Pool sweepstakes.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump, AstraZeneca unveil drug pricing breaks for US consumers
item.description: The president said the U.K.-based company will list many of its most popular drugs online at TrumpRx, a new website.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: John Lodge, singer and bassist of Moody Blues during classic era, dies at 82
item.description: LONDON (AP) — John Lodge, the singer and bassist of British rock band The Moody Blues for more than 50 years, has died. He was 82. In a statement Friday, his family said "with the deepest sadness” that Lodge died “suddenly and unexpectedly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Remains discovered in backyard container are of a young girl: Police
item.description: NEW BRITAIN, Conn. (WTNH) — New Britain police Friday confirmed the human remains found in a large container in the backyard of an abandoned home on Wednesday are that of "an adolescent female.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, NewsNation

item.title: Air traffic controllers who duck unpaid work during the gov't shutdown could be fired, Duffy warns
item.description: Even though the U.S. has a stark shortage of air traffic controllers, ones who call in sick instead of working without a paycheck during the federal government shutdown risk being fired, the U.S. transportation secretary warned.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Firings of federal workers begin as White House seeks to pressure Democrats in government shutdown
item.description: The White House budget office said Friday that mass firings of federal workers have started in an attempt to exert more pressure on Democratic lawmakers as the government shutdown continues.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man sentenced for releasing noxious gas cloud at Meijer
item.description: A man will spend years in prison after a cloud of noxious gas was released during a theft at a grocery store in Michigan.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rosendale, NewsNation

item.title: Blast at a Tennessee explosives plant leaves 18 missing and feared dead, sheriff says
item.description: Officials say an explosion at a Tennessee explosives plant has left 18 people missing and feared dead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Watch: House Republicans give remarks on shutdown Day 10
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) and House Republican leaders gave remarks Friday morning as the government shutdown stretched into a 10th day.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Bryan Kohberger caught on camera calmly shopping at stores hours after Idaho student murders
item.description: Surveillance video shows Bryan Kohberger shopping hours after killing four University of Idaho students before his guilty plea resulted in life sentences.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alex Jones asks Supreme Court to halt $1.4 billion defamation judgement
item.description: Conspiracy theorist Alex Jones urged the Supreme Court on Thursday to immediately halt a $1.44 billion defamation judgment he owes for falsely claiming the Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting was a hoax.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Lyft scraps feature that let drivers see how often riders tip
item.description: Lyft has scrapped a test feature that showed some drivers how often passengers tip before accepting a ride.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rutgers professor signs petition to disband Turning Point USA from campus
item.description: A Rutgers professor signed a Change.org petition accusing the conservative Turning Point USA chapter of promoting hate speech and inciting violence against the community.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump does not win Nobel Peace Prize despite Israel-Hamas deal and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Troops will miss paychecks next week without action on the government shutdown
item.description: The nation’s third shutdown in 12 years is once again raising anxiety levels among service members and their families because those in uniform are working without pay.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Pope urges news agencies to stand as bulwark against lies, manipulation and post-truths
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Leo XIV encouraged international news agencies on Thursday to stand firm as a bulwark against the “ancient art of lying” and manipulation, as he strongly backed a free, independent and objective press.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Tesla self-driving software under investigation after traffic violations
item.description: The NHTSA pointed to 18 complaints and one media report in which Tesla vehicles using the self-driving software failed to remain stopped or to stop fully at a red light or failed to accurately detect the traffic signal.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Most states with National Guard troops in DC plan to withdraw this fall
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than half the states contributing National Guard troops to President Donald Trump's federal law enforcement initiative in Washington have set target dates for their withdrawal later this fall, state officials told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Small businesses feeling pinch of government shutdown
item.description: When the government shuts down, the Small Business Administration stops processing new loans and approving routine small-business loans.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Your phone rings, and it's a number from Sweden. Do you answer? A Nobel Prize winner didn't
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — For some Nobel Prize winners this year, the news came with a knock at the door before dawn. For others, it was a long-awaited phone call honoring a discovery made decades ago.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO and ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, Fox 5
